title: hieracium juranum subsp. benthamianum
intro: Hieracium juranum subsp. benthamianum, also known as bentham's hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium juranum plant, which belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Bentham's hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
